steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The task is to emphasize a similarity between the two specimens: Jane (a Tyrannosaurus) and Fafnir (a Triceratops). The correct choice must bring both specimens together around a shared characteristic from the notes.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Both the Tyrannosaurus fossil specimen Jane and the Triceratops fossil specimen Fafnir lived in the Late Cretaceous Period.: Directly names both specimens and identifies the one trait they share — living in the Late Cretaceous Period. This is the similarity. ✓
  • Choice B — While the Tyrannosaurus fossil specimen Jane is in Rockford, Illinois, the Triceratops fossil specimen Fafnir is in Saint Paul, Minnesota.: Contrasts the two specimens by location rather than emphasizing a similarity. ✗
  • Choice C — The Burpee Museum of Natural History is home to Jane, a Tyrannosaurus fossil specimen from the Late Cretaceous period.: Mentions only Jane; Fafnir is absent. Does not compare two specimens. ✗
  • Choice D — Dinosaur fossil specimens can be found at science museums all over the world, including in Rockford, Illinois, and Saint Paul, Minnesota.: Makes a general claim about museums, not a direct statement about Jane and Fafnir specifically. ✗
That is the correct answer.
